title: Fix bug whereby --get-login doesn't do anything
body:
The  `--get-login` option for `buildah login` doesn't work because it incorrectly defaults to true and the if statement that performs the action checks the value is false.  This fix swaps them round so it defaults to false and the if statement checks it's true.

Test case:

buildah login --get-login *registry*


Signed-off-by: Paul Urwin <register@paulurwin.com>

comments.body:
Can one of the admins verify this patch?
I understand the following commands:
- `bot, add author to whitelist`
- `bot, test pull request`
- `bot, test pull request once`
bot, add author to whitelist
bot, retest this please
@QiWang19 PTAL

Thanks @QiWang19 !
And also thanks @PaulUrwin for the PR.  Git however doesn't like the length of the title.  Can you shorten it up a bit please?

What happens if I do 
```
buildah login --get-login=false docker.io
```
Versus
```
buildah login --get-login=true docker.io
```

Same as Podman with the proposed change.  But honestly I'm not sure why get-login should be false and what it should do in that case?

```
# buildah login --get-login docker.io
tomsweeneyredhat
# buildah login --get-login=false docker.io
tomsweeneyredhat
# buildah login --get-login=true docker.io
tomsweeneyredhat

# podman login --get-login docker.io
tomsweeneyredhat
# podman login --get-login=false docker.io
tomsweeneyredhat
# podman login --get-login=true docker.io
tomsweeneyredhat

```
My understanding would be --get-login=false would prompt for the user name.

Our document doesn't describe `--get-login` as a bool value that can be true or false. I think `--get-login` is just an option, if `buildah/podman login --get-login docker.io` return username, if `buildah/podman login docker.io`, it will do the normal login.
Ok as long as we define it that way, I am fine with it.
LGTM
Thanks for the Git pointers, I didn't realise what a Git newb I am!
@rh-atomic-bot r+
